title: helosciadium ammi
intro: Helosciadium ammi, also known as Indian celery or Ajwain, is a biennial herb native to the Mediterranean region and India, characterized by its fern-like foliage and small, greenish-white flowers. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including its expectorant and anti-inflammatory effects, which can help alleviate respiratory issues and digestive problems. The health benefits of Helosciadium ammi include its ability to relieve coughs, colds, and flu, as well as its anti-bacterial and anti-fungal properties, which can aid in wound healing and skin infections. Herbal preparations of Helosciadium ammi include teas, infusions, and decoctions, often used in combination with other herbs to enhance its medicinal properties.
